title: Implement workspace roles and server-side authorization

environment.os: Windows local; GitHub Actions Ubuntu and Windows

commands.name: Missing workspace authorization module
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/workspace-authorization.test.ts --project integration tests/integration/workspace-authorization.test.ts
commands.expectedFailure: The workspace authorization module did not exist

commands.name: Production browser acceptance and visual verification

scenarios.scenario: A privacy officer assigns a reviewer role
A test operator cannot assign workspace roles
A workspace outside the active tenant is indistinguishable from unavailable

assets.altText: Privacy officer success state showing a reviewer role assignment and append-only authority events.
assets.caption: A server-authorized ROLE_ASSIGN request succeeds for a fictional privacy officer and is recorded in the active workspace audit.

assets.altText: Test operator denial state showing that role assignment is blocked and recorded.
assets.caption: The same ROLE_ASSIGN request is denied for a fictional test operator; audit content remains unavailable to that role.

assets.altText: Narrow-screen test operator denial state with readable controls and locked audit details.
assets.caption: The denied authorization journey remains usable and legible at a 390-pixel viewport.

assets.altText: Privacy officer audit view showing the test operator role-assignment denial and its reason.
assets.caption: After switching to a review-capable role, the caller workspace shows the recorded ROLE_MISSING denial with human actor provenance.

assets.altText: Generic workspace-unavailable response with a denial recorded in the caller's active workspace.
assets.caption: A known ID from another fictional tenant reveals no target name or user and returns the same generic unavailable response.

proofs.description: Workspace creation, permission matrix, role denial, and forged-principal audit isolation
Seeded append-only provenance and workspace-isolation properties
PostgreSQL cross-workspace read and export isolation
Signed-session and server authorization checks for every AUT-01 HTTP route category
Gherkin allowed, denied, and cross-workspace user journeys
Playwright step definitions, exact expected-denial checks, console gate, and visual capture
Explicit permission matrix, authorization service, and in-memory/PostgreSQL repositories
HMAC-signed HTTP-only fixture session containing identity but no role claims
Cross-platform Next.js and Cucumber browser acceptance runner
Ubuntu and Windows Chromium installation before deterministic verification
Curated AUT-01 verification record and bounded limitations

knownLimitations: The web surface is an explicitly labelled local fixture with signed fictional sessions and is not yet connected to an external identity provider.
The authorization service has a PostgreSQL repository and integration coverage, while the deterministic browser fixture intentionally uses an in-memory repository.
The evidence verifies the implemented access-control contract; it is not a third-party security certification.
